verses.commentary: After Jesus said this, He looked toward heaven and prayed: Father, the hour has come. Glorify Your Son, that Your Son may glorify You. High Priestly Prayer begins. Eyes heavenward. The hour arrives. Mutual glorification requested. Son's glory enables Father's glory.

For You granted Him authority over all people that He might give eternal life to all those You have given Him. Authority over all flesh. Purpose: give eternal life. To given ones. Divine sovereignty in salvation. Election evident.

Now this is eternal life: that they know You, the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom You have sent. Definition of eternal life. Ginōskōsin (γινώσκωσιν), know. Relational knowledge. True God. Jesus Christ sent. Knowing God and Jesus is life itself.

I have brought You glory on earth by finishing the work You gave Me to do. Mission accomplished. Edoxasa (ἐδόξασα), aorist, I glorified. Telesios (τελειώσας), finishing. Cross imminent but work counted as done. Anticipatory completion.

And now, Father, glorify Me in Your presence with the glory I had with You before the world began. Request for re-glorification. Pre-incarnate glory. Before creation. Return to original state. Exaltation prayer.

I have revealed You to those whom You gave Me out of the world. They were Yours; You gave them to Me and they have obeyed Your word. Revelation accomplished. Given ones identified. Ek tou kosmou (ἐκ τοῦ κόσμου), out of the world. Father's possession transferred to Son. Obedience marks them.
Now they know that everything You have given Me comes from You. Recognition achieved. All Jesus has is from Father. Source acknowledged.

For I gave them the words You gave Me and they accepted them. They knew with certainty that I came from You, and they believed that You sent Me. Words transmitted. Received. Knowledge and belief. Certainty of origin. Faith in mission.

I pray for them. I am not praying for the world, but for those You have given Me, for they are Yours. Specific intercession. Not for world. For given ones. They belong to Father. Particularity of prayer.

All I have is Yours, and all You have is Mine. And glory has come to Me through them. Mutual ownership. Everything shared between Father and Son. Disciples glorify Jesus. Their faith brings Him honor.

I will remain in the world no longer, but they are still in the world, and I am coming to You. Holy Father, protect them by the power of Your name, the name You gave Me, so that they may be one as We are one. Departure versus remaining. They stay in hostile world. Prayer for protection. Divine name as guardian. Unity requested. Modeled on Trinitarian unity.

While I was with them, I protected them and kept them safe by that name You gave Me. None has been lost except the one doomed to destruction so that Scripture would be fulfilled. Past protection. Jesus guarded them. None lost. Except Judas. Son of perdition. Apolleia (ἀπώλεια), destruction. Psalm 41:9, 109:8 fulfilled.

I am coming to You now, but I say these things while I am still in the world, so that they may have the full measure of My joy within them. Joy purpose. Prayer spoken for their benefit. Full joy. Peplērōmenēn (πεπληρωμένην), completed. Jesus' joy in them.

I have given them Your word and the world has hated them, for they are not of the world any more than I am of the world. Word given. Hatred follows. Non-belonging to world. Like Jesus, set apart. Otherness produces hostility.

My prayer is not that You take them out of the world but that You protect them from the evil one. Not removal but protection. They remain in world. Guarded from Satan. Ek tou ponērou (ἐκ τοῦ πονηροῦ), from the evil one. Preservation, not extraction.
They are not of the world, even as I am not of it. Reiteration. Non-belonging. Jesus and disciples share alien status. In world, not of it.
Sanctify them by the truth; Your word is truth. Hagiasson (ἁγίασον), sanctify, set apart. Truth as agent. Word is truth. Progressive holiness through Scripture.
As You sent Me into the world, I have sent them into the world. Mission parallel. Jesus sent by Father. Disciples sent by Jesus. Apostolic commissioning. Into hostile world.
For them I sanctify Myself, that they too may be truly sanctified. Jesus' self-consecration. For their sake. His setting apart enables theirs. Sacrificial sanctification. Cross sanctifies.

My prayer is not for them alone. I pray also for those who will believe in Me through their message. Extended scope. Future believers included. Through apostolic word. All subsequent Christians. Prayer spans centuries.

That all of them may be one, Father, just as You are in Me and I am in You. May they also be in Us so that the world may believe that You have sent Me. Unity prayer. Model: Trinitarian oneness. Believers in Father and Son. Purpose: world's belief. Unity is evangelistic.
I have given them the glory that You gave Me, that they may be one as We are one. Glory given. Shared divine glory. For unity. Oneness through glory.

I in them and You in Me, so that they may be brought to complete unity. Then the world will know that You sent Me and have loved them even as You have loved me. Indwelling. Teteleiōmenoi (τετελειωμένοι), perfected, completed. Perfect unity. World's knowledge goal. Jesus' mission validated. Father's love for believers equals love for Son. Astounding claim.

Father, I want those You have given Me to be with Me where I am, and to see My glory, the glory You have given Me because You loved Me before the creation of the world. Desire expressed. Thelō (θέλω), I will, desire. Presence request. See His glory. Pre-creation love. Eternal relationship.

Righteous Father, though the world does not know You, I know You, and they know that You have sent Me. Dikaie Pater (δίκαιε πάτερ), righteous Father. World's ignorance. Jesus' knowledge. Disciples' knowledge of mission. Contrasting knowledge.

I have made You known to them, and will continue to make You known in order that the love You have for Me may be in them and that I Myself may be in them. Revelation mission. Past and future. Purpose: Father's love in disciples. Jesus in disciples. Love and presence imparted.
